steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the function of the underlined portion. The underlined text appears inside dashes after "This approach has some limits" — so its role is to specify what those limits are. Any correct answer must describe the underlined text as naming or explaining a limitation.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘It describes the imprecise and subjective nature of neighborhood boundaries.’: The underlined text says the Places API is restricted to customer-facing places — it says nothing about neighborhood boundaries being imprecise or subjective. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘It introduces a model that a research team built to evaluate the mix of amenities in urban neighborhoods.’: The model is introduced in the first sentence. The underlined text is a limitation of the data source, not an introduction of the model. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘It emphasizes the potential utility of the team's model.’: The underlined text is explicitly a limitation, not a positive claim about utility. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘It identifies a specific flaw in using a data set about amenities in cities.’: The underlined text states that Places API data are restricted to customer-facing places — a concrete flaw in the data set used to study neighborhood amenities. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
